title: McCormick & Company (MKC) Intrinsic Value
description: An independent discounted cash flow (DCF) intrinsic value analysis of McCormick & Company (MKC) compiled entirely by Gemini 3.1.

fcfGrowth.title: FCF Growth Rate (Y1-Y5)
fcfGrowth.rationale: The 5.0% growth rate assumption reflects our balanced view on the company's ability to grow free cash flow over the next 5 years.

discountRate.title: Discount Rate (WACC)
discountRate.rationale: A 7.5% discount rate is applied to reflect the risk profile and cost of capital.

terminalGrowth.title: Terminal Growth Rate
terminalGrowth.rationale: The 2.5% terminal growth rate is consistent with long-term macroeconomic expectations.

thesisParagraphs: McCormick & Company is a classic defensive stock within the Packaged Foods industry, offering a portfolio of essential consumer staples. As the world's largest producer of spices, its dominant position provides a wide economic moat supported by strong retailer relationships and brand loyalty. However, the current macroeconomic environment has tested its pricing power, leading to volume pressures as consumers become increasingly price-sensitive.

Our valuation reflects a conservative outlook, anticipating modest 5% free cash flow growth from its current $564M base as the company navigates these headwinds. With a forward P/E of around 16x and a dividend yield of 3.55%, the current valuation appears to fully price in its defensive qualities, leaving limited margin of safety for investors at current levels.
verdictFooter: Based on FY2025 actuals. Data via S&P Global (Mar 20, 2026).

faq.question: Why is McCormick's growth rate estimated at 5%?
faq.answer: A 5% growth rate balances the company's historical performance and recent 2.9% revenue growth with current volume pressures, assuming continued pricing actions and cost savings.

faq.question: What justifies the 7.5% discount rate?
faq.answer: The 7.5% discount rate reflects McCormick's relatively low beta of 0.57 and defensive characteristics as a consumer staple, offset by some macroeconomic risks.

faq.question: Is McCormick a good defensive play?
faq.answer: Yes, its products are essential and it offers a 3.55% dividend yield, but current valuations may already reflect this defensive premium.
